title: NOC 14404 – Dispatchers | Canada Immigration with GTR Immigration
description: Dispatchers play a vital role in coordinating emergency responses, transportation fleets, utility crews, and logistics operations across private, public, and government sectors in Canada. Classified under NOC 14404, this occupation has experienced strong post-pandemic hiring growth and is expected to remain in steady demand—creating practical immigration pathways through employer-supported work permits, Provincial Nominee Programs (PNPs), and long-term Permanent Residence (PR) strategies.

classification.teer: Expansion of public-safety servicesGrowth in logistics and delivery networksEntry-level TEER occupation with specialization tracksEmployer-supported recruitmentInfrastructure and utilities development
classification.major_group: 144 – Supply chain logistics, tracking, and scheduling coordination occupations
classification.minor_group: 1440 – Supply chain logistics, tracking, and scheduling coordination occupations

duties: Dispatchers may perform some or all of the following:
Receive emergency or service requests and coordinate response teams
Transmit instructions using computer-aided dispatch systems
Deploy personnel based on schedules or emergencies
Advise drivers on route, traffic, and weather conditions
Operate radio systems for remote operations
Maintain payroll, mileage, fuel, and service records
employment_requirements: Provincial radio-operator certification for emergency dispatch roles
Formal on-the-job training for police and emergency dispatchers
Informal training for commercial dispatch roles
job_outlook: Between 2022 and 2031, Canada is projected to create approximately 18,100 new positions for Dispatchers, while about 15,200 job seekers are expected—making provincial nomination strategies, employer sponsorship, and targeted immigration planning especially valuable for overseas applicants.
additional_information: Dispatchers are in demand across emergency services, transportation companies, utilities, and industrial operations nationwide. Hiring has accelerated since the pandemic and is projected to continue over the coming years.
Police, fire, and ambulance servicesHospitals and emergency call centresTaxi, courier, and trucking companiesUtility providersMining, forestry, and offshore operations
Annual salaries typically range between $31,800 and $77,100 CAD, depending on province, experience, and sector.
